Appearances: Michael Stecher appeared via zoom for Sherri Stieg (petitioner).

Mr. Stecher stated that there were no objections to this petition and that Mr. Murray agreed to the everything in the petition. Amended petition of Sherri Stieg for approval of final account is taken under submission.

Off Calendar Sep-26-2023 Continued To Nov-14-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Mike Stecher appeared via zoom for Sherri Steig (petitioner).
Thomas Murray appeared via zoom for and with Troy and Shelly Moats (co-administrators).

Mr. Stecher stated that the funds need to be distributed to the new executors, that he is working on the accounting but will need additional time, and that he will be able to clear the examiners notes within thirty days. Final accounting and Petition by resigning executor is continued to November 14, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Papers to be filed by October 30, 2023.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Cabrera. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-10-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance via Zoom: Thomas Murray, Esq, appearing for Troy Moats and Shelley Moats (Petitioners/Proposed Co-Administrators).

The Court addressed Mr. Murray stating that his clients are in Kentucky, that he is in San Diego, and that the court is here in San Francisco. Mr. Murray stated that he has not handled cases in San Francisco and noted that he has only reviewed the local rules as they have come up when related to his filings. The Court noted that there are lots of local rules for the San Francisco Superior Court and advised Mr. Murray to get up to speed. The Court stated that it was ready to rule and addressed the need for co-administrators. Mr. Murray responded stating that Shelley Moats is Troy Moat's wife, that Ms. Moats would like to help her husband, and that they have a minimum bond ready to go.

There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The lost will dated January 11, 2018, was previously admitted to probate by the order filed March 8, 2022. Troy Moats and Shelley Moats are appointed as the Co-Administrators of the estate with full authority. Bond is set at $20,000.00. Amy Harrington remains the probate referee. Order for the same signed in open court. Status on close of the estate is set for January 31, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed two weeks prior, the status hearing will be taken off calendar.

The Court orders the March 28, 2023, status on close of estate off calendar.

Status on the filing of a final account by Sherri Stieg as resigning executor, pursuant to Probate Code 10952, is set for March 29, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Final Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Oct-11-2022 Continued To Nov-22-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sherri Stieg, via Zoom, in propria persona, Executor.

Ms. Steig reports on the issues causing delays in closing this matter and advises that she can no longer serve as Executor and requests to resign accordingly. The court advises Ms. Steig that she will need to file a written, formal request to resign. Ms. Steig reports of advising decedent's family regarding her intent to resign but has not received notice of any interest to serve as Executor. The court and Ms. Steig discuss the status of the Estate. The court advises Ms. Steig to file a request for resignation and provide notice as required.

The court continues the Status hearing to Nov-22-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status of Resignation is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of A Final Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Jul-26-2022 Continued To Oct-11-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-08-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sherri Stieg, via ZOOM, in propria persona, Petitioner.

The court notes and waives the defect in the published notice. The court and notes and waives the defect in the mailed notice. The court confirms with the petitioner that she understood everything listed in the Duties and Liabilities. The court confirms with the petitioner that she cannot open a separate estate account and will be required to keep assets segregated and fully insured. The court confirms with petitioner that she will be responsible to pay valid creditors and will not make any distributions unless ordered by the court.

No one appeared to object. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The lost will dated Jan-11-2018 is admitted to probate. Sherri Stieg is appointed Executor of the estate with limited authority. Bond is not required. Amy Harrington is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on the Filing of a Final Inventory and Appraisal is set for Jul-26-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on close of estate is set for Mar-28-2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed by Mar-14-2023, the status hearing will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Gomez Not reported.
